attacking_style: Opportunistic late attacks on uphill drags and breakaway exploitation in reduced bunch scenarios. Shows willingness to attack from breakaways rather than waiting for sprint finishes.
team_tactics: Lotto positioning skills evident in reduced bunch situations, though appears to operate with some autonomy in breakaway scenarios rather than dedicated leadout support.
terrain_preference: Uphill drags and transition stages that reduce pure sprinter numbers. Struggles less in reduced bunch sprints than flat mass gallops, exploiting positioning over pure speed.
vulnerability: Limited Grand Tour breakaway experience until late career suggests unfamiliarity with multi-week racing dynamics. Pure flat sprints against full sprinter contingents not preferred terrain.
key_patterns: Breakaway specialist who finishes rather than bridges: podium results from breakaways twice observed
Prefers reduced bunch scenarios over mass sprints, attacks late rather than contests full sprint
Late-career Grand Tour breakaway debut suggests tactical evolution after nine professional seasons
Uphill finish preference: attacks on drags to surprise pure sprinters
form_trajectory: neutral: 2021 Tour breakaway showed continued willingness but no tactical advancement from 2019 Vuelta performances
